title: Make Account Insights Actionable for Enterprise Reporting
excerpt:

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Account Insights provides a helpful high-level view of activity across users, boards, automations, integrations, and storage. However, it currently stops at surface-level visibility and does not provide the depth needed for operational or executive reporting.

Key limitations today:

  1. Flexible time controls
    Yearly view, custom date ranges, and the ability to align to company-specific fiscal quarters

  2. Export and unified dataset
    Ability to export all insights, not just certain pages, into a single dataset for reporting

  3. Segmentation and filtering
    By workspace, team, department, and user role to support accountability

What are you trying to achieve

The goal is to turn Account Insights from a visibility dashboard into a true reporting and decision-making tool.

Specifically, we need to be able to:

Report on usage and adoption over time, including yearly trends and company-specific fiscal quarters

Export a complete and consistent dataset for analysis and executive reporting

Drill into metrics to identify which teams, boards, and users are driving results or issues

Segment data by workspace and organizational structure to support accountability

Ultimately, we want to be able to clearly answer:
“Is this platform delivering enough value to justify the investment?”

Right now, Account Insights provides visibility, but not enough depth to confidently answer that question.
